svg.title: Resident Off-Chain Projection Pipeline
svg.desc: Diagram showing how the bot bootstraps local state, decodes sequencer feed messages, mutates resident data, recomputes health factor and gates runtime decisions.
svg.text: Resident Off-Chain Projection Pipeline
Sequencer feed events are projected on top of a bootstrapped local Aave state, then gated by confidence before the runner consumes them.
1. Bootstrap a real local baseline
Aave RPC snapshot seeds the resident store
Block and timestamp, reserve list, reserve configuration, indexes, rates, caps, flags, oracle prices, user account data, user reserve data, EMode.
Balances are normalized into scaled balances so later local math can reuse the same Aave index mechanics.
scaledATokenBalance = currentATokenBalance / liquidityIndex
scaledVariableDebt = currentVariableDebt / variableBorrowIndex
2. Keep resident state in memory
The per-user store keeps:
blockState, reserveStates, priceStates, userReserveStates, userAccountStates, eModeCategories
It also keeps drift, confidence, last feed seq, last block, and recent local decision metadata.
This is the local baseline that future feed events mutate.
3. Decode the sequencer feed
Each feed message becomes an advance-block event first.
Then the L2 batch is expanded into transactions and mapped into engine events only when relevant for the tracked user.
Supported today: pool calls, aToken transfers, supported direct price updates.
Unsupported OCR transmit payloads are counted, but they do not mutate local prices yet.
4. Advance time and mutate local state
Before applying user actions, the engine advances reserve indexes to the feed timestamp.
liquidityIndex_t = accrueLinearIndex(liquidityIndex_0, liquidityRateRay, dt)
variableBorrowIndex_t = accrueBinomialCompoundedIndex(variableBorrowIndex_0, variableBorrowRateRay, dt)
Then event mutators update scaled supply, scaled debt, collateral flags, EMode, reserve prices, and estimated liquidation effects.
Touched users are marked dirty and scheduled for local recomputation.
5. Rebuild each position from scaled state
supplyWei = scaledATokenBalance * liquidityIndex / RAY
variableDebtWei = max(scaledVariableDebt * variableBorrowIndex / RAY, variableDebtMinWei)
stableDebtWei = actualStableDebtWei or principalStableDebt
collateralBase = supplyWei * priceBase / 10^decimals
debtBase = totalDebtWei * priceBase / 10^decimals
weightedCollateralBase = collateralBase * effectiveLtBps / 10000
EMode-aware liquidation threshold is applied when the reserve and user share the same category.
6. Aggregate account state and confidence
totalCollateralBase = sum(collateralBase)
totalDebtBase = sum(debtBase)
healthFactor = weightedCollateralBase * 1e18 / totalDebtBase
If debt is zero, HF is promoted to MAX_HEALTH_FACTOR.
The current projectedHealthFactor is the locally recomputed HF at the latest known feed frontier.
Confidence is reduced by drift, stale data, complexity, stable debt, isolation mode, event gaps, or missing price.
That score decides whether the runner can trust the local state.
7. Runtime gate
Confidence and freshness above the configured threshold?
Main gates: RESIDENT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD and RESIDENT_MIN_CONFIDENCE_BUILD_STATE
YES: resident-first monitoring and hybrid reads
Critical and recheck can read local decisions without monitoring RPC.
NO: defer or use RPC fallback where still allowed
Execution paths still keep RPC for canonical validation, quotes, submit, and receipt.
Current runtime policy
Sequencer feed updates resident state,
but does not trigger extra fast scan,
recheck, or oracle RPC loops.
